channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Russia Launches 400 Drones in Daytime Attack on Ukraine
item.description: Russia launched over 400 drones at Ukraine in a daytime attack, injuring 10 people in the western city of Ternopil. The mass assault marks one of the largest drone barrages of the war.

item.title: White House Declares Iran War "Terminated" as War Powers Deadline Hits
item.description: The Trump administration told Congress the Iran war has been "terminated" as a legal deadline arrived requiring presidential notification. The move aims to avoid seeking congressional authorization.

item.title: Trump "Not Satisfied" With Iran's Latest Peace Proposal
item.description: President Trump said he was not satisfied with Iran's latest proposal for talks to end the war, without specifying what was unacceptable. Iran's foreign minister said Tehran remains ready for diplomacy.

item.title: Atlantic Alliance Creaks as US-Europe Split Deepens Over Iran
item.description: US-European relations face strain after European reluctance to support the Iran campaign. NATO eastern flank nations continue training with US forces despite diplomatic tensions.

item.title: Russian Drone Strike Damages Odesa Port Infrastructure
item.description: A Russian drone attack overnight damaged port infrastructure in Ukraine's southern Odesa region and wounded two people in the city, according to regional officials.

item.title: Trump to Raise EU Auto Tariffs to 25%
item.description: President Trump announced he will increase tariffs on cars and trucks from the European Union to 25% next week, accusing the bloc of not complying with its trade deal with Washington.

item.title: Ukraine Announces Army Reform as Peace Talks Stall
item.description: Ukraine unveiled plans to reform its army this summer to address infantry shortages and discharge long-serving soldiers, four years into the war with Russia as diplomatic efforts have stalled.

item.title: Iranian Crypto Exchange Used by IRGC to Move Millions
item.description: A Reuters investigation reveals that one of Iran's most powerful families founded Nobitex, the country's largest crypto exchange, which has become a central node for moving funds beyond US sanctions—even during the current war.

item.title: Transatlantic Frost Deepens as Trump Attacks European Leaders
item.description: The past weeks have not been reassuring for those who thought Europe could navigate its tricky relationship with Trump, with the president threatening troop withdrawals and tariff hikes while criticizing European inaction on Iran.

item.title: Investors Running Out of Time to Brace for Oil Shock
item.description: With markets focused on AI and hoping for a short-lived Iran war, investors have yet to prepare for a potential doubling of physical oil prices. The window to do so may soon be closing.

item.title: US Sanctions Iranian Shadow Banking Networks Moving Oil Proceeds from China
item.description: The US Treasury targeted three Iranian foreign currency exchange houses and front companies that facilitated transferring oil proceeds from China to Iran, aiming to cut off Tehrans access to hard currency.

item.title: Japan Refiner Buys Russian Oil as Middle East Alternative
item.description: Taiyo Oil is purchasing crude from Russia to diversify supply away from the conflict-hit Middle East, reflecting growing Asian energy concerns as the Iran-US war disrupts traditional supply routes.

item.title: Lebanon Death Toll Surpasses 2,600 Since March 2 Israeli Offensive
item.description: Lebanons Health Ministry reports 2,618 deaths and over 8,000 injuries from Israeli attacks since March 2, as the regional spillover from the Iran-US conflict continues to escalate.

item.title: Ukrainian Firm Unveils Mid-Range Drone to Exhaust Russian Air Defenses
item.description: General Cherrys Khmarynka drone enters a growing class of low-cost, mid-range strike weapons designed to deplete Russian air defense stocks, modeled on Russias own Molniya design.

item.title: Oil Rally Stalls as Iran Proposal Triggers Profit-Taking
item.description: Crude prices headed for a weekly close below after wild swings, as Irans negotiation proposal cooled markets while traders locked in gains from the conflict-driven surge.

item.title: Iran submits new peace proposal to US via Pakistani mediators
item.description: Tehran has handed over a revised proposal for negotiations with the United States, Iranian state media and a Pakistani official confirmed, potentially offering a path to break the deadlock in ending the Iran war.

item.title: US Navy taps AI firm to counter Iranian mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US Navy is accelerating AI capabilities to detect Iranian mines in the critical Strait of Hormuz shipping lane, according to a newly awarded contract with AI firm Domino.

item.title: France24: Iran supreme leader vows to protect nuclear and missile capabilities
item.description: As Iran delivered a new proposal for US talks, Supreme Leader Khamenei defiantly pledged to protect the Islamic Republic nuclear and missile programs, which Washington has demanded be dismantled.

item.title: Japan Eases Weapons Export Rules, Opening Path for Ukraine Arms Supplies
item.description: Japan relaxed its weapons-export rules, creating a pathway for potential military equipment supplies to Ukraine. Kyiv's ambassador to Japan said the move opens doors for talks that could help Ukraine resist Russia's invasion.

item.title: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Tuapse Port, Creating Environmental Disaster
item.description: Ukrainian drones struck Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse for the fourth time in 16 days, causing toxic black smoke clouds and oil leaking into the sea. Authorities are struggling to cope with the mounting environmental crisis.

item.title: Lebanon's Internal Divisions Complicate Saudi Mediation With Israel
item.description: A rift among top Lebanese officials has hindered Saudi efforts to help Lebanon forge a united position on negotiations with Israel. The internal splits come as ceasefire talks between Israel and Hezbollah remain fragile.

item.title: Commodity Trader Mercuria Sues Baltic Exchange Over Hormuz Shipping Data
item.description: Commodity trader Mercuria is suing the Baltic Exchange over losses from oil tanker pricing data that failed to account for the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The lawsuit highlights the financial fallout from the Iran crisis.

item.title: California Gasoline Hits $6 Per Gallon Amid Iran Crisis
item.description: Motorists in California are paying $6 per gallon for gasoline, the highest in two years, as the Strait of Hormuz standoff drives global oil prices higher. The price surge is becoming a political flashpoint ahead of elections.

item.title: Pakistan commissions first Chinese attack submarine in historic milestone for navy
item.description: Pakistan inducts advanced Hangor-class submarine built by China, strengthening naval capabilities amid growing defense ties between Islamabad and Beijing.

item.title: Bahrain king orders more citizenship revocations over support for Iran attacks
item.description: Bahrain's King Hamad orders additional citizenship revocations for individuals accused of supporting Iranian attacks, escalating the Gulf state's crackdown amid regional tensions.
